title: Bain M&A Study 2025-26
relevant_snippets: – Deals >$5B contributed 75% of strategic deal value growth; most (~60%) by infrequent acquirers and ~40% were “transformative” in size (>50% acquirer market cap)
• The great M&A rebound of 2025 is on track to deliver the second highest total deal value ever ($4.8T), up +36% in value, 5% in count
– More investment is going to capex (36%) and R&D (19%); the Magnificent 7 alone spent a combined ~$500B in capex and R&D through Q325 (versus ~$9B on M&A)
private acquirors include privately held and government-owned entities; frequent acquirors made 10+ acquisitions in last 10 years Source: Dealogic as of 17th November 2025, CapIQ
– In 2025YTD, M&A accounted for 7% of S&P World Index companies’ cash expenditures versus a range of 9-17% in preceding 9 years
Strategic, 38% Financial Investors, 31% Venture capital / corporate venture capital, 28% 2025 estimate

Note: Mega deals include strategic acquisitions over $5 billion USD deal value, announced January 1 - November 15th 2025, excludes spin-offs; market cap. determined 20 days prior to deal announcement;
